aximar.title: Interactive Plotting
aximar.description: Interactive Plotly.js charts with ax_plot2d, ax_draw2d, and ax_draw3d.

cells.source:
# Interactive Plotting with Aximar

Aximar provides `ax_plot2d`, `ax_draw2d`, and `ax_draw3d` for interactive Plotly.js plots. These functions use the same draw-package object syntax you already know (`explicit`, `parametric`, `points`, `implicit`) but render as interactive charts you can pan, zoom, and hover over.

| Aximar (interactive) | Maxima (static gnuplot) |
|---|---|
| `ax_plot2d(...)` | `plot2d(...)` |
| `ax_draw2d(...)` | `draw2d(...)` |
| `ax_draw3d(...)` | `draw3d(...)` |

## Quick Start: ax_plot2d

The simplest way to plot. Same syntax as `plot2d` — just an expression and a range.
ax_plot2d(sin(x), [x, -%pi, %pi])
Multiple functions on the same axes — pass a list of expressions.
ax_plot2d([sin(x), cos(x), sin(x) + cos(x)], [x, -2*%pi, 2*%pi])

## Styling Traces

Style options use **Plotly-native names** rather than gnuplot/draw conventions. Options apply to all subsequent traces until changed.

| Aximar option | draw equivalent | Effect |
|---|---|---|
| `color` | `color` | Line/marker color |
| `line_width` | `line_width` | Line thickness |
| `dash` | `line_type` | Dash pattern: `"solid"`, `"dot"`, `"dash"`, `"dashdot"` |
| `marker_symbol` | `point_type` | Marker shape: `"circle"`, `"diamond"`, `"square"`, ... |
| `marker_size` | `point_size` | Marker size |
| `fill` | `filled_func` | Fill region: `"tozeroy"`, `"tonexty"`, `"toself"` |
| `opacity` | — | Trace opacity (0–1) |
| `name` | `key` | Legend label |

ax_draw2d(
  color="red", line_width=3, name="x²",
  explicit(x^2, x, -3, 3),
  color="blue", dash="dash", name="x³",
  explicit(x^3, x, -3, 3),
  title="Polynomials", xlabel="x", ylabel="y"
)

## Parametric Curves

Same `parametric(x(t), y(t), t, t₀, t₁)` syntax as the draw package.

ax_draw2d(
  color="purple", line_width=2,
  parametric(cos(t), sin(t), t, 0, 2*%pi),
  title="Unit Circle",
  aspect_ratio=1
)
A Lissajous figure — try hovering over the curve to see coordinates.

ax_draw2d(
  color="teal", line_width=2, nticks=1000,
  parametric(sin(3*t), sin(4*t), t, 0, 2*%pi),
  title="Lissajous (3:4)"
)

## Scatter Data

Plot discrete data points with `points`. Marker style is controlled with `marker_symbol` and `marker_size`.

ax_draw2d(
  color="crimson", marker_symbol="diamond", marker_size=10,
  points([[1,1],[2,4],[3,9],[4,16],[5,25]]),
  name="Data",
  title="Scatter Plot",
  xlabel="n", ylabel="n²"
)

## Implicit Curves

`implicit(equation, x, x₀, x₁, y, y₀, y₁)` renders level-set contours — useful for curves that can't be written as y = f(x).

ax_draw2d(
  implicit(x^2 + y^2 = 4, x, -3, 3, y, -3, 3),
  title="Circle x² + y² = 4",
  aspect_ratio=1
)

## Combining Multiple Object Types

Mix explicit curves, points, and implicit contours in a single plot. Style options apply to each subsequent object.

ax_draw2d(
  color="steelblue", line_width=2,
  explicit(sin(x), x, -%pi, %pi),
  color="orange", dash="dot",
  explicit(cos(x), x, -%pi, %pi),
  color="red", marker_size=8,
  points([[-2, 0.5], [0, 1], [2, -0.3]]),
  title="Mixed Plot"
)

## Filled Regions

Use `fill="tozeroy"` to shade the area between a curve and the x-axis.

ax_draw2d(
  color="mediumseagreen", fill="tozeroy", opacity=0.4,
  explicit(exp(-x^2), x, -3, 3),
  title="Gaussian Bell Curve (filled)"
)

## 3D Surfaces

`ax_draw3d` produces interactive 3D plots you can rotate and zoom. Drag to orbit, scroll to zoom.

ax_draw3d(
  explicit(sin(x)*cos(y), x, -%pi, %pi, y, -%pi, %pi),
  title="sin(x)·cos(y)",
  xlabel="x", ylabel="y", zlabel="z"
)
A saddle surface — hover over the surface to read coordinates.

ax_draw3d(
  explicit(x^2 - y^2, x, -2, 2, y, -2, 2),
  title="Saddle: x² − y²"
)
## 3D Scatter Data

ax_draw3d(
  marker_size=5, color="darkorange",
  points([[1,1,1],[2,2,8],[3,3,27],[4,4,64],[5,5,125]]),
  title="Cubic Growth"
)

## Plotting Computed Results

Compute a derivative symbolically and plot both the function and its derivative.

f: x^3 - 3*x$
df: diff(f, x)$
ax_draw2d(
  color="navy", name="f(x) = x³ − 3x",
  explicit(f, x, -3, 3),
  color="red", dash="dash", name="f'(x) = 3x² − 3",
  explicit(df, x, -3, 3),
  title="Function and Derivative",
  xlabel="x", ylabel="y"
)

## Tips

- **Pan & zoom**: drag to pan, scroll to zoom, double-click to reset
- **Hover**: hover over any point to see exact coordinates
- **Legend**: click a legend entry to hide/show that trace
- **3D rotation**: drag to orbit, scroll to zoom, ctrl+drag to pan
- Use `nticks=N` to control sampling resolution (default 500 for 2D, 50 for 3D)
- The original `plot2d`/`plot3d` still work for static gnuplot SVGs
